title.en: Causative Verbs: make, let, have, get

summary.en: Causative verbs mean 'make / let / get someone to do something'. They take an object plus an object complement, and the four most common ones — make, let, have, get — each carry a slightly different shade of meaning.
summary.zh: 使役动词表达“使、让、令某人做某事”。它们后面除了接宾语，还要再加宾语补语；最常见的四个——make、let、have、get——各自的隐含意义略有不同。

heading.en: An object alone is not enough

body.en: Verbs like know, eat, and teach express a complete idea once you add an object — know the fact, eat carrots, teach grammar. But with a verb like 'make', adding only an object leaves the idea unfinished: 'I make the wolf' is not a complete thought. You still have to say what the object does, so this kind of verb needs an object plus an object complement.
body.zh: 像 know、eat、teach 这样的动词，后面加个宾语就能表达完整意思——know the fact、eat carrots、teach grammar。但像 make 这样的词，光加宾语句意还不完整：“I make the wolf”（我使狼）会让人觉得话没说完。你还得交代宾语做了啥，所以这类动词除了宾语，还需要宾语补语。

concepts.sourceRef: 光是在之后加个宾语，这里的宾语是狼，句意还不完整

body.en: Verbs that need both an object and an object complement come in several classes; one class means 'make / let / cause someone to do something'. Grammatically these are called causative verbs. The four most representative ones are make, let, have, and get — master these and the rest follow easily.
body.zh: 需要后接宾语和宾语补语的动词有好几类，其中一类表达的含义是“使、让、令”。这一类动词在语法上被称作使役动词。最有代表性的四个是 make、let、have、get——掌握好这几个，其他的使役动词就很好理解了。

concepts.sourceRef: 这一类动词在语法上被称作使役动词

heading.en: The complement can take many forms

body.en: The object complement after a causative verb can be a bare infinitive (a to-infinitive with the 'to' dropped), an adjective, an adverb, or a past participle. The bare infinitive only looks like a base verb — it is really a non-finite verb, so the sentence still has only one predicate verb.
body.zh: 使役动词后的宾语补语可以是裸不定式（去掉 to 的不定式）、形容词、副词或过去分词。裸不定式只是长得像动词原型，其实是非谓语动词，所以一句话里仍然只有一个谓语动词。

concepts.sourceRef: 动词原型或者说裸不定式、形容词、副词、过去分词等

body.en: 'Make' as a causative verb implies forcing or requiring someone to do something; it is not the 'manufacture' sense of 'made in China'. It is followed by an object plus a bare infinitive.
body.zh: make 作使役动词时，隐含意义通常是迫使或需要某人做某事；它不是“made in China”里制造的意思。它后接宾语加裸不定式。

concepts.sourceRef: Make的隐含意通常是迫使某人做某事

body.en: 'Let' implies allowing or permitting someone to do something. Like 'make', it is followed by an object plus a bare infinitive. The complement can also be an adverb, as in 'Let me out', where 'out' is an adverb acting as the object complement.
body.zh: let 的隐含意义是允许某人做某事。和 make 一样，它后接宾语加裸不定式。补语也可以是副词，如“Let me out”，这里的 out 是副词，作宾语补语。

concepts.sourceRef: Let的隐含意通常是允许某人做某事

heading.en: have — assign a task / get something done
heading.zh: have — 给予任务 / 让人把事办好

body.en: 'Have' has many roles (possession, auxiliary), but as a causative it implies giving someone a task or responsibility: 'I'll have the wolf wash the dishes' (object + bare infinitive). You can also use 'have + object + past participle' — 'I had my car washed' — where the doer is unspecified and the participle reads passively. The same pattern can also describe misfortune: 'I had my carrot stolen yesterday.'
body.zh: have 身份很多（表示“有”、做助动词），但作使役动词时隐含意义是给某人一个任务或责任：“I'll have the wolf wash the dishes”（宾语 + 裸不定式）。也可以用“have + 宾语 + 过去分词”——“I had my car washed”——施动者不明，过去分词带被动含义。这个句型也能表达遭遇不幸：“I had my carrot stolen yesterday”。

concepts.sourceRef: Have的隐含意通常是给某人某项责任或者说任务

heading.zh: get — 说服 / 鼓励（要带 to）

body.en: 'Get' as a causative implies persuading or encouraging someone to do something. Crucially, its object is followed by a full infinitive — the to-infinitive — not a bare infinitive: 'I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen.' Don't drop the 'to'.
body.zh: get 作使役动词时隐含意义是说服或鼓励某人做某事。关键是它的宾语后要接完整不定式，即带 to 的不定式，而不是裸不定式：“I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen”。千万别漏了 to。

concepts.sourceRef: 请注意 "get" 的宾语后要接完整动词不定式 full infinitive，即带 "to" 的不定式

examples.text: I make the wolf eat carrots from time to time.

note.en: 'Make' forces the wolf into the action. 'The wolf' is the object; the bare infinitive 'eat' is the object complement that completes the meaning.
note.zh: “make”迫使狼做这个动作。“the wolf”是宾语；裸不定式“eat”是补足句意的宾语补语。

examples.text: I'll let the wolf eat some chocolate.

note.en: 'Let' = allow. After 'let', the complement is again a bare infinitive ('eat'), with no 'to'.
note.zh: “let”表示允许。在“let”之后，补语仍是裸不定式（“eat”），不加 to。

examples.text: I'll have the wolf wash the dishes.

note.en: Here 'have' is not 'possess'. It assigns the wolf the task of washing the dishes — object + bare infinitive.
note.zh: 这里的“have”不是“有”。它把洗盘子的任务交给狼——宾语 + 裸不定式。

examples.text: The carrot made the wolf full.

note.en: The object complement can be an adjective: 'full' describes the resulting state of the object 'the wolf'.
note.zh: 宾语补语可以是形容词：“full”描述宾语“the wolf”被造成的状态。

note.en: 'Out' here is an adverb (not a preposition) serving as the object complement.
note.zh: 这里的“out”是副词（不是介词），作宾语补语。

note.en: 'have + object + past participle': the doer is unspecified and 'washed' is passive — 'my car was washed by someone as I wanted.'
note.zh: “have + 宾语 + 过去分词”：施动者不明，“washed”是被动——“my car was washed by someone as I wanted”。

examples.text: I had my carrot stolen yesterday.

note.en: Same 'have + object + past participle' shape, but here it signals misfortune — not a task or service, and certainly not the result I wanted.
note.zh: 同样是“have + 宾语 + 过去分词”，但这里表示遭遇不幸——不是任务或服务，更不是我想要的结果。

examples.text: I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen after she washes the dishes.

note.en: 'Get' implies persuading/encouraging. Its complement is a FULL infinitive 'to clean' — never drop the 'to'.
note.zh: “get”隐含说服/鼓励之意。它的补语是完整不定式“to clean”——绝不能漏掉 to。

prompt.en: Which causative verb requires a FULL infinitive (with 'to') after its object?
prompt.zh: 哪个使役动词的宾语后要接完整不定式（带 to）？

explain.en: 'Get' takes a full infinitive — 'I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen.' 'Make' and 'let' take a bare infinitive (no 'to').
explain.zh: “get”接完整不定式——“I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen”。“make”和“let”接裸不定式（不带 to）。

prompt.en: What does causative 'have' imply in 'I'll have the wolf wash the dishes'?
prompt.zh: 在“I'll have the wolf wash the dishes”中，使役动词 have 隐含什么意思？

text.en: Possessing the wolf and the dishes
Assigning the wolf the task of washing the dishes
text.zh: 把洗盘子的任务交给狼

text.en: Forcing the wolf against its will

explain.en: Causative 'have' has nothing to do with 'possess'; it implies giving someone a task or responsibility.
explain.zh: 使役动词 have 和“有”无关；它隐含给某人一项任务或责任之意。

prompt.en: Complete the causative pattern: 'I'll get the wolf ____ wash my car.'
prompt.zh: 补全使役结构：“I'll get the wolf ____ wash my car.”

explain.en: 'Get' needs a full infinitive, so insert 'to': 'I'll get the wolf to wash my car.'
explain.zh: “get”需要完整不定式，所以要加 to：“I'll get the wolf to wash my car”。

prompt.en: Rewrite as 'have + object + past participle': turn 'I'll have the wolf wash the dishes' into 'I'll have the dishes ____.'
prompt.zh: 改写为“have + 宾语 + 过去分词”：把“I'll have the wolf wash the dishes”改成“I'll have the dishes ____.”

explain.en: Dropping the doer, the complement becomes a past participle reading passively: 'I'll have the dishes washed.'
explain.zh: 省去施动者后，补语变成带被动含义的过去分词：“I'll have the dishes washed”。

quizzes.sentence: I make the wolf to eat carrots.
quizzes.fix: I make the wolf eat carrots.

explain.en: 'Make' takes a bare infinitive, so there is no 'to' before 'eat'. Only 'get' uses the full infinitive.
explain.zh: “make”接裸不定式，所以“eat”前不加 to。只有“get”用完整不定式。

prompt.en: In 'The carrot made the wolf full', what kind of object complement is 'full'?
prompt.zh: 在“The carrot made the wolf full”中，“full”是哪种宾语补语？

explain.en: 'Full' is an adjective describing the resulting state of the object 'the wolf'. Causative complements can also be bare infinitives, adverbs, or past participles.
explain.zh: “full”是形容词，描述宾语“the wolf”被造成的状态。使役补语也可以是裸不定式、副词或过去分词。

title.zh: 一只兔子，四个使役动词

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: I make the wolf eat carrots from time to time.

note.zh: make = 迫使；宾语 + 裸不定式。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Fine. But then let me eat some chocolate.

note.zh: let = 允许；宾语 + 裸不定式。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Deal. And I'll have the wolf wash the dishes.

note.en: have = assign a task; object + bare infinitive.
note.zh: have = 给予任务；宾语 + 裸不定式。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Then I'll get the wolf to clean the kitchen too.

note.en: get = persuade; note the FULL infinitive 'to clean'.
note.zh: get = 说服；注意完整不定式“to clean”。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: And tomorrow I'll have my car washed by you!

note.en: have + object + past participle: doer added with 'by you', participle reads passively.
note.zh: have + 宾语 + 过去分词：用“by you”补出施动者，过去分词带被动含义。
